reproduction_summary: Attempted SDAR reproduction (Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning, arXiv 2605.15155). Code was successfully generated implementing the sigmoid-gated OPSD algorithm (g_t=σ(β·Δ_t)), stop-gradient on gate, all 5 baselines (GRPO/OPSD/Skill-SD/GRPO+OPSD/RLSD), and 4 skill-retrieval strategies. Both run attempts failed: (1) all search_qa/webshop cells hit CUDA OOM because a pre-existing process occupies 22+ GiB of the 23.68 GiB A5000 GPU, leaving insufficient memory for any Qwen model; (2) ALFWorld cells hit AttributeError on missing build_student_prompt method. WebShop data endpoint returned HTTP 404. The paper requires 8×H800 GPUs; this hardware is unobtainable. An improvement attempt (smaller batches + gradient checkpointing) was implemented and re-run but encountered identical OOM failures — the bottleneck is the pre-existing process, not batch size. Rubric score: 0.00 (target: 0.60). Verdict: failed.

config.scale_note: Trained on 1×RTX A5000 (25.4 GB). Paper used 8×H800. batch_size reduced from paper: search_qa 128→16, alfworld 16→4, webshop 16→8.

areas.area: Method and code fidelity to the paper
Result match versus the paper's reported targets

improvements.title: Fix Silent OOM with Sharding and Smaller Batches
Restore Paper Constants and Real Qwen Weights

scope.requested: Qwen3-1.7B + Qwen2.5-3B on ALFWorld/WebShop (smallest two variants)
gaps: All search_qa/webshop cells: CUDA OOM on 1×A5000 (23.68 GiB) — another process holding 22+ GiB; Qwen models cannot be loaded (unobtainable without GPU eviction or multi-GPU)
ALFWorld: ALFWorldEnv.build_student_prompt method missing — interface bug in generated code
Paper required 8×H800 for training; single A5000 is insufficient for any model size
